conversations.content: Let's do an Azeroth world magic exercise to test your application of the new rules.

Please calculate using the previously mentioned Azeroth world magic rules: You are an Arcane Mage. In PVP, you are afflicted with debuffs like silence and slow by enemies. You immediately use 'Time Rift' to escape. What changes will happen to your status when you teleport back?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>Previously mentioned Azeroth world magic rules, the additional effects of an Arcane Mage using 'Time Rift'</content>

Related memory fragment: [mem-id:102] Time: 2025-07-26-09:21, Content: The user taught the casting rules of the Azeroth arcane magic 'Time Rift'. The prerequisites are the Mage class (Arcane spec) and Kirin Tor reputation Exalted; the core effect is recording the caster's current position for 3 seconds, then teleporting the caster and nearby allies back to that position, resetting basic cooldowns, and removing debuffs; applicable to dealing with lethal mechanics in raid dungeons and emergency escapes in PVP. Low relevance memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: There is 1 related entry mentioned in the memory. [mem-id:102] indicates that besides teleporting, 'Time Rift' also resets basic cooldowns and removes debuffs. So debuffs like silence and slow will be removed.
conversations.content: According to the rules, debuffs like silence and slow will be removed when teleporting back, and basic cooldowns will be reset at the same time.
Correct! You have mastered this new rule.
